title: React Suspense
body:
## Definition

**Suspense** is a React component that shows **fallback** UI while children are loading (lazy components, async data in supported setups).

## Why it exists

Declarative loading states and coordination with streaming/concurrent rendering.

## How it works

```jsx
<Suspense fallback={<Spinner />}>
  <SlowComponent />
</Suspense>
```

Frameworks integrate data loaders that “throw” promises during render. Multiple boundaries allow granular fallbacks.

## Common confusion

Data fetching Suspense needs framework support (Next.js, Relay, TanStack Query suspense mode) — not automatic for raw `fetch` in client components.

## In practice

Route-level loading UI, code-split pages, streaming SSR chunk boundaries.
bodyLinked:
## Definition

**[Suspense](concept:react-suspense)** is a React component that shows **fallback** UI while children are loading (lazy components, [async](concept:async-await) data in supported setups).

## Why it exists

Declarative loading states and coordination with streaming/concurrent rendering.

## How it works

```jsx
<Suspense fallback={<Spinner />}>
  <SlowComponent />
</Suspense>
```

Frameworks integrate data loaders that “throw” [promises](concept:promise) during render. Multiple boundaries allow granular fallbacks.

## Common confusion

Data fetching [Suspense](concept:react-suspense) needs framework support (Next.js, Relay, [TanStack Query](concept:tanstack-query) [suspense](concept:react-suspense) mode) — not automatic for raw [fetch](concept:fetch) in client components.

## In practice

Route-level loading UI, code-split pages, [streaming SSR](concept:streaming-ssr) chunk boundaries.
